title: The Scanner Function
description: Learn to use scanner() to check crop status and grid states before acting.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: One of the most powerful functions is scanner(x, y). It lets you inspect any grid position without moving there!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: It returns data about what's at that position - the grid type, crop status, and available functions.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: This is essential for writing efficient code. Check before you act!

objectives.description: Use scanner() to inspect a farmland position

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Good! The scanner returns an object with status info. You can check if crops are 'ready' for harvest.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: There's also can_harvest() - stand on farmland and it returns True/False if the crop is ready.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: This is perfect for conditional logic - you'll use this a lot with if statements!

objectives.description: Use can_harvest() to check crop status

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! These inspection functions will be critical when you start using loops and conditionals for smart farming.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now for a practical challenge: scan multiple farmland positions, then harvest only the ready crops.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: You'll need to: scan positions, check their status, move to ready ones, and harvest. Think it through!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Excellent work! You're using scanner() like a pro. Next, let's talk about the upgrade system.
